rationale: In the citing paper, Naik and Raman (2003) is used as an example within a broad review of diagnostic practices in JMR articles, including a note that it reports an explicit exogeneity test. It appears in grouped evidence tables and a brief illustrative sentence, alongside many other studies. The citing paper does not develop its own model, hypotheses, or policy-simulation framework from that specific study, nor does it rely on its synergy findings for core argumentation.
